def: A concave surface, or the space bounded by it; the state ofbeing concave.
A form of silicon carbide, produced in the electric furnace,possessing great hardness, and high electrical resistance, and notsubject to oxidation below 2880º F., or 1600º C.
The small, blueblack, drupelike fruit of the Nuttalliacerasiformis, a shrub of Oregon and California, belonging to theCherry tribe of Rosaceæ.
A rake for collecting hay; especially, a large rake drawn by ahorse or horses.
The quality or state of being intangible; intangibleness.
A plain cap or headdress for women or girls; especially, onetying under the chin by a very broad band, generally of the samematerial as the cap itself. Thackeray.
Possessed of the properties of chyle; consisting of chyle.
A large fire built in the open air, as an expression of publicjoy and exultation, or for amusement.Full soon by bonfire and by bell, We learnt our liege was passingwell. Gay.
A lady; madam; mistress; -- the title given a lady in Italy.
